sanskrit:
कामात्मानः स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम् ।
क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति ॥ ४३ ॥
transliteration:
kāmātmānaḥ svarga-parā janma-karma-phala-pradām |
kriyā-viśeṣa-bahulāṁ bhogaiśvarya-gatiṁ prati || 43 ||
literal_meaning: Desire-driven selves, heaven-oriented, promising birth-and-action fruits, rich in ritual specifics, heading toward enjoyment-and-opulence.
contextual_meaning: Krishna exposes the spiritual materialist: people who use religion as a consumer product to buy heavenly pleasure, status, and rebirth comfort. They mistake ritual busy-work for inner growth.
psychological_state: Narcissistic spirituality—using sacred language to defend against existential dread while secretly craving sensory and social rewards.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling Instagram wellness influencers who promise 7-figure incomes and ‘higher vibrations’ if you buy their $999 manifestation course.
anchor_text: Don’t trade worship for rewards; seek self-knowledge, not heavenly merchandise.
